data.title: Stifel total client assets flat M/M in April despite market volatility
data.description:
Stifel Financial (NYSE:SF) total client assets were $486B at April 30, 2025, essentially flat with March 31, and up 7% from $454B on April 30, 2024, the company said on Thursday.

Fee-based client assets of $191B also were roughly flat from the prior month, but increased 11% Y/Y.

"Total client assets and fee-based assets increased 7% and 11%, respectively, from the same period a year ago, due to market appreciation and our continued success in recruiting productive financial advisors," said Chairman and CEO Ronald J. Kruszewski. "On a month-on-month basis, both our total client assets and fee-based assets finished relatively in-line with March levels, despite significant volatility in the equities markets."

Client money market and insured products, which includes Smart Rate deposits, sweep deposits, third-party bank sweep program, and other sweep cash, fell 5% M/M and 1% Y/Y to $26.1B. The declines in Smart Rate and sweep deposits were largely due to typical seasonality.

Net bank loans of $21.5B increased 1% M/M and 8% Y/Y.

Stifel (NYSE:SF) stock slid 1.3% in after-hours trading.

data.title: Ross Stores sets tariff-impaired Q2 guidance, pulls FY25 outlook
data.description:
Ross Stores reported a top-line beat in the first quarter but warned that tariffs will likely shave 11 to 16 cents off second quarter profits, spooking investors and sending shares sharply lower in Thursday’s after-hours trading.

“Heightened macroeconomic and geopolitical uncertainty persists, most notably prolonged inflation and evolving trade policies. While we directly import only a small portion of our merchandise, more than half of the goods we sell originate from China. As such, we expect pressure on our profitability if tariffs remain at elevated levels,” said Ross CEO Jim Conroy.

Given the uncertain environment, the company is only issuing Q2 guidance and has pulled its previously provided full year outlook.

For the quarter ending August 2, comparable store sales are expected to be flat to up 3% versus a 4% increase in the same quarter last year. Earnings are expected to be between $1.40 to $1.55 per share, down from $1.59 in Q2 2024 and includes a cost impact of $0.11 to $0.16 from announced tariffs. This is as much as 26 cents less than expectations for a profit of $1.66 per share.

For the reported quarter, Ross Stores (NASDAQ:ROST) earned a profit of $1.47 per share, a penny better than a year ago and 4 cents above expectations. Total revenue increased by 2.5% to $4.98B, in-line with expectations. Comparable store sales were flat, versus estimates for a contraction of 0.9%.

“The company has been aggressively opening new stores this year which invariably drives top-line growth,” said Kidsy CEO Shraysi Tandon, adding that at a time when most consumer and retail companies are pausing on expansion, “discount retailers like Ross are the few that can confidently expand their penetration.”

Despite the pressure from tariffs, Tandon expects Ross (NASDAQ:ROST) will continue to see top-line growth.

“With a robust pipeline of new store openings, I believe ROST is well positioned to see meaningful top-line acceleration this year.”

Ross Stores (NASDAQ:ROST) shares are down 9% in after-hours trading.

data.title: Occidental Petroleum continues losing streak to seventh straight session
data.description:
Occidental Petroleum (NYSE:OXY) extended losses for the seventh session Thursday after its shares inched down 0.12% to end at $41.08.

The energy giant’s shares lost over 6% in the preceding six sessions and they closed nearly 2% lower at $41.13 on Wednesday. On a year-to-date basis, the company has lost more than 18% of its value compared to a 0.6% fall in the broader S&P500 index.

Looking at Seeking Alpha’s quant ratings, it is recommended to  Hold OXY. It has a score of 2.92 out of 5 with an A+ for profitability and D+ for valuation. Wall Street analysts also echo a similar sentiment rating the stock as Hold.

However, Seeking Alpha analysts suggest to  Buy Occidental’s stock. Nine analysts have rated the company a Buy or higher while three recommend to Hold and one had a Sell call.

Earlier this month, the company posted mixed Q1 results where it beat earnings estimates but missed revenue projections by $110M.

Following the Q1 print, Seeking Alpha analyst Oakoff Investments  said that the results were a testament to the oil producer’s strong cash flow position.

“While the firm's revenue of ~$6.803B came in slightly below Wall Street's consensus of $6.91 billion (Q1 marked the second top-line miss in a row), I think the real story lies in the earnings and cash flow generation,” the analyst said.

However, another analyst Michael Boyd  argued that although free cash flow from non-oil projects is expected to improve in 2026, substantial shareholder returns are unlikely before 2027.

“Valuation spreads are tight, and I prefer investing in larger, more diversified supermajors over Occidental for both oil and non-oil growth,” Boyd said in his article.

data.title: Rio Tinto selected for second major Chile lithium project
data.description:
Rio Tinto (NYSE:RIO) said Thursday it was selected by Chile's government for the second time this week for a major new lithium project, with the state-run mining company Empresa Nacional de Minería naming it a partner for its Altoandinos lithium project.

Rio (NYSE:RIO), which would acquire an initial 51% stake in the project while ENAMI holds the remaining 49%, will provide an estimated $425M including a pre-feasibility study, use of its pilot plant at the Rincon project in Argentina, and the use of its direct lithium extraction technology; ENAMI said the project will represent a total investment of $3B.

Earlier this week, Rio (RIO) also was selected by state-run copper miner Codelco for the Maricunga lithium project, agreeing to contribute up to $900M in the deal.

With a strategic pipeline of tier 1 lithium assets and options, including those from the recent acquisition of Arcadium Lithium, Rio Tinto (RIO) now holds one of the world's largest lithium portfolios.

data.title: LPL Financial advisory and brokerage assets inch down 0.4% M/M in April
data.description:
LPL Financial's (NASDAQ:LPLA) total advisory and brokerage assets totaled $1.79T at the end of April, down 0.4% from the end of March, it said on Thursday. That's still up 27% from a year ago.

Total organic net new assets for April were $6.1B, translating to a 4.1% annualized growth rate. 

Prior to the impacts of $0.2B of assets that off-boarded in April as part of the previously disclosed planned separation from misaligned large OSJs, alongside $0.1B of assets from Wintrust Investments, LLC and certain private client business at Great Lakes Advisors, LLC, organic net new assets were $6.2B, translating to 4.1% annualized growth.

That's well below the $13.1B recorded in March and $7.0B logged a year earlier.

Total acquired net new assets were $0 in April, compared with $7.1B in March and $5.0B in April 2024.

Total clients cash balances fell 2.4% sequentially and climbed 13.3% Y/Y to $51.8B.
